title: Determination of total glutathione levels in Plasmodium falciparum infected RBCs
author: Unknown
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.i35cgq6

steps.title: step 1 Preparations of PfRBCs for...
steps.instruction: step 1 Preparations of PfRBCs for total GSH and GSSG estimation: Harvest PfRBCs*/RBCs (5.4 x 10 8 cells) in cultures and washed with phosphate-buffered saline. *at 5% parasitemia Disrupt the packed PfRBCs/RBCs by three freeze-thaw cycles. Add 100 µl 5% (w/v) sulfosalicylic acid solution (SSA) to the disrupted PfRBCs/RBCs, and vortex. Collect the supernatants by centrifugation for 5 min at 8,000g at 4°C, and transfer to 500 µl chilled, ultrapure water* (final volume 600 µl). *Wako Pure Chemical Industries, Osaka, Japan, catalogue no. 210-01303

steps.title: step 2 Quantification of total glutathione...
steps.instruction: step 2 Quantification of total glutathione (GSH + GSSG) and GSSG by using the total Glutathione Quantification kit or the GSSG/GSH Quantification kit*. *total Glutathione Quantification kit (catalogue no. T419) and GSSG/GSH Quantification kit (catalogue no. G257) Dojindo Molecular Technologies, Inc., Rockville, MD, USA

steps.title: step 3 Quantification of total glutathione...
steps.instruction: step 3 Quantification of total glutathione (GSH + GSSG) and GSSG using the GSSG/GSH Quantification kit (G257) For total GSH estimation, prepare GSH standard solutions in 0.5% SSA (50, 25.0, 12.5, 6.25, 3.13, 1.57, 0 µM). For GSSG estimation, prepare GSSG standard solutions in 0.5% SSA (25.0, 12.5, 6.25, 3.13, 1.57, 0.78, 0 µM). Add 4 µl masking solution to 200 µl GSSG standard solutions, and 200 µl samples prepared by the procedure above (step 1) in a micro tube, and mix well. Place 40 µl of GSSG standards, GSH standards, and samples in a 96-well microtiter plate. Add 60 µl Buffer solution to each well. Incubate the plate at 37 °C for 1 h. Add 60 µl of Substrate working solution to each well. Add 60 µl of Enzyme/coenzyme working solution to each well, and mix well. Incubate the plate at 37 °C for 10 min. Read the absorbance at 405 nm (OD 405 ) on a microplate reader, and extrapolate concentrations of total GSH and GSSG from each standard curve.
